title: Reconciliation
description: The reconciler compares the current component tree against saved state and computes the minimal diff.
step_render_body: the component tree is evaluated, producing a fiber tree with instance nodes
step_load_state_body: the previous state is loaded from Memory
step_diff_body: the reconciler compares previous and current instance nodes by ID (path-based)
step_dependency_graph_body: builds a parent-child dependency graph from node paths
step_topological_sort_body: orders nodes so parents deploy before children
step_apply_body: handlers run for created and updated nodes, cleanup runs for deleted nodes
step_save_state_body: the new state is persisted to Memory
change_detection_intro: The reconciler detects three types of changes:
change_creates: Creates: nodes in the current tree whose ID wasn't in the previous state
change_deletes: Deletes: nodes in the previous state whose ID isn't in the current tree
change_updates: Updates: nodes present in both, whose props differ (deep equality check)
matching_body: Nodes are matched by their id, which is derived from the path (e.g., app.aws.web-site-blog). There is no type-based matching.
deep_equal_body: Props are compared using deep equality (deepEqual). Identical props, even new object references, won't trigger a handler re-run.
parallel_deployment_intro: After computing the change set, the reconciler builds a dependency graph from path hierarchy (parents must deploy before children) and groups independent nodes into parallel batches. The ChangeSet includes:
parallel_deployment_batches: parallelBatches: groups of nodes that can be deployed concurrently
source_intro: The reconciliation logic lives in runtime/src/reconcile.ts. Key exports:
source_build_dependency_graph: buildDependencyGraph(): builds parent-child dependency graph from node paths
source_compute_parallel_batches: computeParallelBatches(): groups independent nodes for concurrent deployment
